The Official BNI Podcast

A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Episode 647: BNI Is More than Referrals

February 26, 2020 by Ivan Misner 18 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

Lorena Medina, National Director of BNI Mexico, joins Dr. Misner on the podcast this week to share a story that shows BNI is more than referrals.

One of the biggest challenges that entrepreneurs have is that no one ever taught them how to run a business. They go to school to learn a profession, but only teaches them how to be a dentist or a doctor or a lawyer—not how to be a business owner.

BNI is not a business school. The mission of BNI is to help members grow their businesses through referrals. But learning how to run a business can be a side effect of joining BNI.

Lorena has seen this at work. A dentist named Lupita went to a BNI meeting as a visitor. As she listened to each member’s presentation, she realized that she didn’t have a business name, or a logo, or incorporation papers, or many other things that she needed. She knew what to do once the patient was sitting in the chair in front of her, but not how to get them there. That’s what Lupita saw she could learn in BNI, and she’s been a member for 10 years now.

If you’ve experienced a positive “side effect” of joining BNI, share your story in the comments.

Complete Transcript of BNI Podcast 647

Priscilla:
Hello, everybody and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, California. And I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner.

Hello Ivan. How are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I am doing fantastic and this week I am back in the Southern California area speaking in the founding regions of BNI where I started BNI 35 years ago. And Mike Adray is the Executive Director there. And Mike’s putting on an event with a number of the other local directors and I’m really excited to be back in my old hometown and do a presentation.

Priscilla:
Great.

Ivan:
We’re doing this as part of our 35th anniversary in BNI. So today I have a guest and my guest’s name is Lorena Medina. I consider Lorena a friend. She’s also the National Director of BNI Mexico. She’s been in BNI since 2005. She has a degree in International Relations. She lives in Mexico City, and her hobbies include movies, reading, writing, and travel.

And something nobody about her knows–and I didn’t know it until I read this bio–is that she has written a science fiction novel, which she has not published yet. And so I want her to publish it because I love love this. Science fiction!

Lorena’s BHAG, or “Big Hairy Audacious Goal”, is building a community of 100,000 networkers that will change the way Mexico does business. Lorena, it is truly an honor to have you on the BNI Podcast. Thanks for coming on today.

Lorena:
Thank you, Ivan. It’s an honor to be here.

Ivan:
Well, I appreciate you and you are on because, I think it was at a BNI convention, you mentioned to me a story about a dentist in BNI that I thought was amazing. And I asked if you’d share it, and anything else that you have that you’d like to talk about, about how BNI is more than just referrals. So let me hand it over to you, Lorena.

Lorena Medina
Thank you, Ivan. Yeah, we were together at the global convention in Poland last year. And I think that the question that was posed to me was, “In my experience in the last 14 years of running BNI Mexico, what is the biggest challenge that business entrepreneurs run into?” I mean, I have known a lot of businesspeople in my BNI career and I felt that not only in Mexico but in Latin America, one of the main challenges that business owners have today, that’s what I have observed, is that nobody ever really told us how to run a business.

I mean, a lot of us maybe went to college, and maybe if I am a dentist, I went to college, and they taught me everything that I was supposed to do, from the time when I have a patient in front of me with his or her mouth open. But what about everything that surrounds that operation, everything that you’re supposed to have in place to actually run a business, nobody teaches you this unless you’re actually going to business school somewhere. So when this question was posed to me, I thought about it and I said, “You know what, we are all running our business the best way we can and we learn a few tricks along the way.”

But this is actually something that is really exciting to me because this is not our main or core business in BNI. Our business is referrals. Our business is not really to go inside a particular company and teach you how to run your business. However, this happens. I mean, I saw it happen with a very particular story that I use to put an example of how BNI can help you turn your business into something a lot more professional than if you were on your own.

So I met this dentist, her name is Lupita. So she is a member in the first chapter that I launched in Leon in Mexico. And she told me the story of the first time that she went to visit a chapter as a visitor. And she told me that from the first visit that she had in this chapter, she immediately saw value because she started listening to all the presentations from the members of this chapter. And she started realizing all the things that were lacking in her business.

She realized, “I do have a profession, I’m a dentist, but I don’t have a business built around it.” She started realizing, “You know what, I don’t have a logo. I don’t have the incorporation papers for my business. I have never trained any of my employees. Maybe I need a business consultant.”

Or maybe it’s just realizing all of the things that she could do in her business to be better, to actually have a structure that would allow her to grow. And what we do in BNI is actually provide the pipeline for you to get more business, but you need to also have structure to support that growth. So I thought it was fascinating that that’s not even what we do. But it does happen inside the BNI chapter. So we do more than referrals.

Ivan:
And did she pick up some ideas? I’m guessing the answer is yes to that. She picked up some ideas on how to develop a logo, how to incorporate, how to run the business, by surrounding herself with other BNI members.

Lorena:
Yes, exactly. So that was my conclusion as well. So it does help. It does help you as a business person to be more professional and to develop a more professional business by being surrounded by specialists in these other areas. She probably would have never, ever evolved the way she did, she probably would have never had a name for her business, the incorporation papers, the proper hiring process for her people, and a logo, all of these things that she developed by being in the same room with other professionals, with other people that were all very good in their own specialties. So being in a BNI meeting is the right place to be because it’s inevitable that you’re going to learn something and that you’re going to be better as a result.

Ivan:
You know, I don’t know if you know this, one of the founding members of the second chapter of BNI, the chapter that my wife, Elisabeth who you know well, was in, was a dentist and he was a member for 30 years in the group. And I think that’s amazing. And it sounds like this dentist has picked up a lot of great ideas in BNI.

Lorena:
Yeah, that’s exactly what happened, I mean, to this dentist, Lupita that I’m telling her story now, she has been a member for 10 years. And what I would say is that I have been involved in BNI for 14 years, going to be 15 years in November, and every time I go to a BNI meeting, I learn something. And there’s always something that you can learn from the members, and there’s always some profession or some business specialty that you didn’t even know existed, that you start realizing what’s lacking in your own business. So that makes you better. 

Ivan:
It’s not just about referrals, it’s about learning how to run the business more effectively, which isn’t what–it’s not our mission, but I think that’s the point you’re making is that that we still pick up a lot of other ideas in addition.

Lorena:
Exactly. This is one of the, what I call, BNI side effects. It’s not our core business. It’s not exactly what it was designed to do. But it happens anyway. So I think, even if you have a really prosperous business and you have a lot of customers and you think you don’t need referrals, you need to be in a room with other people that you can learn from.

I was trying to talk my brother into joining any organization, even if it’s not mine. Because if the only people that you talk to every day are your employees, or are people that are somehow subordinate to you, you need to surround yourself also with people that are more successful than you so you can learn from them. And this is what happens in BNI chapters: you surround yourself with professionals and they all know something better than you and they all are experts in their areas. And there’s always something that you can learn from them and be better as a result.

Ivan:
Yeah, no question about that. You know, when I started BNI, I was definitely networking up: most of the people that I invited into BNI were more successful than me. But even if you’re in a chapter where that’s not the case, there’s always something to learn from people, from peers, who can teach you something about their business that you didn’t know, that somehow helps you in your business. And this is definitely a side effect of BNI that we really don’t talk about very much and I’m really glad that you brought it up.

What was that expression that the dentist used and you mentioned it at the beginning of this, something about you only making a connection with the people when they’re sitting in front of you with their mouth open? What was it she said?

Lorena:
What she said was, “What they taught me in college was what to do when you already have somebody with their mouth open in front of you, but what happens before we get to that point? How do I get that customer that trusted me enough to be here in front of me with his or her mouth open?” I mean everything that’s surrounding this operation, it’s kind of important too.

Ivan:
And nobody talks about that in dental school, in chiropractic school, and medical school, and legal school and any of those, they don’t talk about how to get people to that point where they’re sitting in front of you with their mouth open. I absolutely love that expression!

Lorena, we’re out of time. But listen, any last thoughts before I turn it over to Priscilla?

Lorena:
Well, just to conclude, I think that this experience that Lupita, my dentist, and the member that I have in my chapter in Leon, it’s a story that probably repeats a lot throughout the organization. So how cool would it be to hear all of these stories of BNI side effects?

Ivan:
That’s a great point. And I think if you’re listening to this and you have a “side effect” like Lorena is talking about, I’d love for you to post that right here on the podcast. Just give us a short version of your story. Comment. Just as a reminder, I read all of the comments that go up there. I’d love to read your comment.

Lorena, really powerful stuff. Thank you so much for being on BNI Podcast today. Priscilla, back to you.

Priscilla:Okay, I think that’s it. Thank you both for the great information. This podcast has a new sponsor. Go check out the great content available at www.ivansinnercircle.com, where Ivan has assembled a wide variety of topics and you are invited to participate.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Special Guest Lorena Medina Posted in Getting The Most From BNI, Special Guests

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Dr Megha Shah says

    February 27, 2020 at 6:18 pm

    My best learning: learnt how to present my medical cosmetology work in easy way, precisely and simply in front of my chapter members, the world’s largest chapter of 156 members, BNI Alchemist, Surat City, Gujarat State, India,.
    Deep gratitude to Dr. Ivan Misner, our executive directors of Surat ‘Mr. Gaurav and Dr. Nidhi Singhvi and Bni Alchemist. I am a member who is 3 plus years old. Dr Megha Shah, Cosmetologist, laser surgeon, Beauty and Curves Skin Laser Clinic.

    Reply

  2. Ing. César González says

    February 27, 2020 at 7:19 pm

    I am also very grateful to BNI for the learning and development with all of the chapter members. I have been able to develop areas of my business that I did not visualize before; and not only in business, but also has made me grow as a person.

    Yo también estoy muy agradecido con BNI por el aprendizaje y desarrollo que he tenido por parte de mis compañeros de capítulo. He podido desarrollar áreas de mi negocio que antes no visualizaba; y no solamente en el negocio si no me ha hecho crecer como persona.

    Reply

  3. Sophie Kiiza says

    February 27, 2020 at 8:18 pm

    Very interesting

    Reply

  4. June Hibbard says

    February 27, 2020 at 11:00 pm

    I have been a BNI member for 19 years. BNI helped me when I had just started my business through to selling it 13 years later. I love the way BNI helps me build my business and the support and knowledge that is available through all the diverse group of people that make up the chapter.

    Reply

  5. Rik Yeames says

    February 28, 2020 at 5:30 am

    My side effect story is over the past 8 years BNI has showed me how to network locally. While my brand is number 1 on the planet, when it comes to local store marketing…not so much. BNI is teaching me how to promote locally as a Logo (Locally Owned Great Operator) Franchisee and become Founder-CVO of the Logo Network, for which I will be forever grateful. With Gratitude, Rik “The pied piper of pizza, peace, & solar eclipses”. :)

    Reply

  6. Alejandro Casas says

    February 28, 2020 at 1:42 pm

    Interesting to see the stories of success are the same no matter where you are located.
    No doubt BNI besides the referrals in an educative marketing tool.

    Reply

  7. David Evans says

    February 28, 2020 at 2:47 pm

    My BNI side effect is similar to the example of the dentist in this podcast and I can add a little more to it as well. I am a commercial real estate broker who is new member to BNI as I was just inducted yesterday 2/26/2020 into the BLAZE chapter in CUMMING,GA. I looked up BNI online after hearing about another residential agent in my office joining a different chapter . As soon as I went to the first meeting as a guest I immediately saw the value of spending regular time other professionals so that their expertise could “rubb-off” onto my business practices as I become influenced by them. The additional side effect I would like to add to the benefit of seeing how other professionals conduct business is FOCUS on the core elements of what needs to be done to make my business better. If I am going to present my business to others on a regular basis I immediately saw that it would hone and focus my efforts on what is most important to get the end results I desire. Thank you! This was my first BNI podcast to listen to.

    Reply

    • Dhammika Jayasuriya says

      March 7, 2020 at 8:27 pm

      Fully Agree BNI is not only referrals.joined Srilankan BNI Chapter some six months back and met two members who really advised me to Rebrand and also make new video advertising a area where I had not much knowledge. The changes have brought me immediate success by my company merging with SRILanka’s biggest organic Rice company.

      Reply

  8. Ma. Guadalupe Cruz Pérez says

    February 28, 2020 at 11:36 pm

    Soy Lupita Cruz del Capitulo Pioneros que menciona Lorena Medina. BNI cambio mi visión de consultorio a una clínica dental.
    Familiarmente mi esposo ha pertenecido a BNI en otro capítulo como endodoncista.
    Mi hija como maestra de español para extranjeros y actualmente mi hija menor pertenece también a Pioneros. Gracias Lorena.Gracias Ing Medina. Gracias BNI.

    Reply

    • Carolina Morillas says

      April 1, 2020 at 2:45 pm

      Qué lindo!!! Toda una familia de BNI! Eso demuestra el gran beneficio que BNI nos da :)

      Reply

  9. Elham jankowski says

    March 1, 2020 at 1:41 am

    Yes I love the expertise that exits in my chapter BNI infinity in Western Australia. I get a lot of ideas from existing members. I was a mathematics teacher until 11 months ago. So a lot of my successful practices came from other members.

    Reply

  10. Wendy Marshall says

    March 1, 2020 at 2:45 pm

    I love this episode. As a Business and Leadership Consultant with 40 years experience in business I am studying Professional Coaching with Australia’s largest and best coaching school The Coaching Institute. Under the guidelines of the International Coach Guild, business certification is a requirement for Professional Coaches accreditation, and I know how important it is to understand how to move from being the technician to entrepreneur

    Reply

  11. Alison Goldsberry says

    March 2, 2020 at 3:05 pm

    Absolutely about Dentists! That is who we help and specialize our advertising campaigns for, health and medical professionals. We give free consultations for our clients because the need is so great! Thanks for sharing everyone. Great episode.

    Reply

  12. Michael Royal says

    March 4, 2020 at 7:39 pm

    Some thoughts:

    BNI is the training ground for SMEs.

    Why? Corporates have expensive training for staff and management which are often too expensive for smaller businesses. This is where BNI steps in. All the things you need to do to make your BNI journey successful are the same things you need to do in your business. I often say to visitors ‘just scrub out the initials BNI on any of our material and see how it fits within your business.’

    What you do in BNI you should be doing in your business.

    Why? A different take on the above but the strategy of specific referral requests is exactly what you should be doing in your buisness – targeted marketing. Same with being ‘present’, being ‘on time’, being ‘a good listener’, learning, making meetings valuable, follow a successful process,the full list at both the personal level and the business level is a long one!

    Reply

  13. Dani Zandel says

    March 6, 2020 at 2:02 pm

    I loved so much of this podcast episode. One point that hit was the variety of professionals in these groups.
    My professional background is in auto dealership management – a grueling industry, time wise, with minimal networking opportunity outside your own employment.
    When I started my own business, the few car folks in my circle were not going to get me far!
    When I joined a networking group, I learned a little about a lot! The members were similar to me in that they knew a ton about their own industry, and by our connections, I got to learn a little from all of them!
    What did this do for me?
    Well, when you’re in a social setting, you never know what conversations may come up… and I found that I became able to participate better because I had learned talking points that I could never have gained from just the people I worked with!
    This is probably one of the best “additional advantages” of networking groups like BNI.

    Reply

  14. Julieta Rushford says

    April 14, 2021 at 5:29 pm

    BINGO! Yes!
    I actually think that it should be illegal to allow students to graduate from chiropractic school (or dentistry, etc) without a business degree as well. BNI comes in very handy for most of us in the healing industry because we despise selling. In BNI, our TEAM sells US! Thank you.

    Reply

  15. Omu Obilor says

    June 14, 2022 at 6:02 pm

    BNI has made me a confident business person. It has made me well-rounded. Improved my networking and presentation skills.

    Reply

  16. Neusa Casado says

    July 16, 2022 at 1:09 pm

    Me identifiquei com Lupila. Aprendo cada dia mais com os membros do BNI. Aprendi muito sobre plantas e projetos pois sou arquiteta/urbanista/paisagista, mas nada sobre como trazer clientes sobre meu negócio. Quando entrei para o BNI nem logotipo tinha, hoje minha empresa está sendo conhecida na cidade, cada dia mais. Ainda tenho muito a melhorar, por isto minha empresa está crescendo a cada dia.

    Reply
